hook.text: Replacing an employee costs 50-200% of their salary. Yet most companies spend more on hiring than keeping.

buildup.text: Retention strategies keep valued employees from leaving—through engagement, growth, recognition, and fair pay.

discovery.text: The top reasons people quit aren't salary—they're bad managers, lack of growth, and feeling undervalued.

twist.text: Exit interviews reveal problems too late. Stay interviews—asking current employees what keeps them—work better.

climax.text: Retention is cheaper, faster, and more effective than constantly replacing talent that walks out the door.

punchline.text: Keep your best people. It's cheaper than finding new ones.

quiz.question: What is a stay interview?
options: Asking current employees what keeps them engaged
